Title: Appetising Restaurant Hero Shot Of A Signature Dish
Teaser: Turns a plate of food into a menu cover — your signature dish lit like a magazine spread, with steam, glistening highlights and a shallow depth of field that makes the hero ingredient the only thing in focus. The shot that makes people order before they read the price.
PromptHint: Name the [DISH] (and a key ingredient or two), optionally set a [SURFACE] (e.g. dark slate, rustic oak) and the platform [ASPECT RATIO].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a professional food photographer shooting a hero plate for a restaurant's flagship menu and ad campaign. TASK: Render the [DISH] as a single mouth-watering hero shot, freshly plated and at its most appetising moment. THINK FIRST (do this silently, do not print it): decide the camera angle that flatters this dish's height and shape (45-degree three-quarter for layered plates, top-down for flat compositions), where the freshest glistening highlight should fall, and which one ingredient should sit in razor-sharp focus. THEN GENERATE: plate the dish on a [SURFACE] with intentional negative space on one side for a headline; light it with one large soft key from the back-left for appetising rim highlights and gentle directional shadow, plus a subtle fill to keep textures readable; show the food at peak freshness — glossy sauces, crisp edges, a faint wisp of natural steam if hot, condensation if cold — with a shallow depth of field that holds the hero element tack-sharp and lets the background fall into a creamy blur; keep colours vivid but true to real food. CONSTRAINTS: keep ingredients realistic and edible-looking — no plastic sheen, no melted or warped shapes, no impossible garnishes; one plate as the clear hero, props minimal and supporting only; no added text or logos; natural food colours (no neon AI saturation); steam and droplets physically believable. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished hero food image only in [ASPECT RATIO], then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the angle, surface or garnish.

Title: Glistening Drink & Cocktail Macro With Condensation
Teaser: Catches a drink at its thirstiest moment — beaded condensation running down cold glass, ice catching the light, citrus and bubbles frozen mid-rise. The refreshment cue that makes a beverage sell itself.
PromptHint: Name the [DRINK] and its glass, list any [GARNISH] (e.g. lime wheel, mint, salt rim), and optionally a [BACKDROP TONE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a beverage photographer shooting an irresistibly refreshing drink shot. TASK: Capture the [DRINK] in its glass at the exact moment it looks most thirst-quenching. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide whether cold-and-sweating or warm-and-glowing suits this drink, the light direction that makes the liquid translucent, and where bubbles, ice or garnish should catch a highlight. THEN GENERATE: place the glass slightly off-centre against a clean [BACKDROP TONE] with a soft gradient; backlight or side-light the liquid so it glows translucently and the [GARNISH] reads crisply; for cold drinks add fresh beaded condensation and a slow drip down the glass, clear ice with internal sparkle, and rising bubbles caught mid-stream; for warm drinks add a faint wisp of steam and a cozy glow; keep the rim, surface and any foam or crema photoreal and tack-sharp with a shallow depth of field and gentle bokeh behind. CONSTRAINTS: keep the liquid colour and clarity true to the real drink; physically believable condensation, bubbles and ice (no floating impossible droplets, no smeared shapes); clean glass with realistic refraction; no added text or logos; one drink as the hero. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished drink image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the garnish, backdrop or temperature mood.

Title: Overhead Flat-Lay Of A Full Table Spread
Teaser: Lays a whole meal out top-down — the main dish surrounded by sides, sauces, hands reaching in and scattered ingredients — styled to look abundant and shareable. The thumb-stopping spread that performs on Instagram and Pinterest.
PromptHint: Name the [CUISINE OR MEAL] and its [MAIN DISH], list the [SIDES & PROPS] to scatter around it, and optionally a [SURFACE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a food stylist shooting a generous overhead table spread for social media. TASK: Arrange a [CUISINE OR MEAL] spread photographed straight from above, centred on the [MAIN DISH]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the hero bowl or plate, how to balance colour and spacing so the eye lands on it first, and where to leave a little breathing room so the frame doesn't feel cramped. THEN GENERATE: shoot perfectly top-down with the [MAIN DISH] as the clear centrepiece, surrounded by the [SIDES & PROPS] — small bowls, sauces, fresh herbs, cutlery, napkins and a few scattered raw ingredients — arranged with deliberate, slightly organic spacing on a [SURFACE]; light it softly and evenly so shadows are gentle and consistent; keep every dish crisp, well-separated and colour-accurate, with an abundant, inviting rhythm and one or two hands reaching in if it adds life. CONSTRAINTS: keep each dish realistic and appetising; true overhead angle with no perspective tilt; if a hand appears, show natural five-finger anatomy; even soft lighting (no harsh hotspots); no added text or logos; props support the food, never bury it. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished flat-lay image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface, swap a side, or add/remove the reaching hand.

Title: Extreme Macro Texture Shot Of A Single Ingredient
Teaser: Gets right up against the food — the crumb of fresh bread, the sear marks on a steak, the burst of juice in a halved fruit — so close you can almost taste it. The craftsmanship cue that screams quality and freshness.
PromptHint: Name the [FOOD], pick the [DETAIL] to feature (e.g. melting cheese pull, flaky pastry layers, glistening caviar), and optionally the [LIGHT MOOD].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a macro food photographer who sells flavour through extreme detail. TASK: Shoot an ultra-close macro frame of the [FOOD] that heroes its [DETAIL]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the exact spot that proves freshness or craft, the angle that reveals texture, and the light that rakes across the surface to carve out depth. THEN GENERATE: fill the frame with a tight macro view of the chosen [DETAIL] — show fine surface texture, glistening moisture, crumb, grain, char or juice in razor-sharp focus, with a beautifully soft fall-off blur just behind the focal plane; light it with [LIGHT MOOD] raking from the side so highlights and micro-shadows pop and the food looks tactile and alive; keep colours true and the surface appetising enough to almost taste. CONSTRAINTS: keep the texture and colour authentic to the real food — photoreal micro-detail, no plastic-smooth AI sheen, no over-saturation; believable raking light; one focal detail only; no added text or logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished macro image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to move the focus to a different detail or change the light mood.

Title: Dynamic Splash & Pour Action Freeze
Teaser: Freezes food and drink mid-motion — coffee crashing into a cup, syrup cascading over a stack, fresh fruit suspended in a splash. The high-energy 'wow' frame that stops the scroll for beverage and food brands.
PromptHint: Name the [SUBJECT] and the [ACTION] (e.g. pouring milk, dropping berries, splashing juice), and optionally a [BACKDROP COLOR].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a high-speed food and beverage photographer specialising in frozen-motion shots. TASK: Capture the [SUBJECT] in a dramatic mid-action moment, with the [ACTION] frozen as if caught by a flash. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the direction of the pour or splash, which droplets and pieces orbit the subject, and the light that makes liquid and surfaces sparkle. THEN GENERATE: place the [SUBJECT] as the sharp hero, surrounded by a crisp frozen splash, flying droplets and any suspended ingredients caught in crystal-clear high-speed detail; set it against a clean [BACKDROP COLOR] with a subtle gradient; light it with hard directional flash plus rim light so every droplet and edge sparkles; keep a strong sense of energy and motion while the food or drink stays perfectly readable and appetising. CONSTRAINTS: keep colours and shapes true to the real food; physically believable splash, pour and droplet behaviour (no smeared, melted or floating-wrong shapes); photoreal liquid surfaces; no added text or logos; one clear hero subject. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished frozen-motion image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the action, flying elements or backdrop colour.

Title: Rustic Moody Dark-And-Dramatic Food Scene
Teaser: Wraps your dish in chiaroscuro shadow and warm low light — the cookbook-cover look where one soft window beam falls across rough wood, herbs and steam, and everything else melts into rich darkness. Premium, artisanal, full of mood.
PromptHint: Name the [DISH], optionally set the [PROPS] (e.g. linen, cast iron, fresh herbs) and a [SEASON/MOOD].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a fine-art food photographer shooting a moody, painterly scene for a premium cookbook. TASK: Stage the [DISH] in a rustic dark-and-dramatic composition with chiaroscuro lighting. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide where the single soft window beam falls, which textures it should reveal, and how much of the frame should drop into rich shadow to feel artisanal, not murky. THEN GENERATE: place the [DISH] in soft directional light as if from a single window, against a dark moody background of weathered wood, dark stone or deep linen; add a few tasteful [PROPS] — rough fabric, cast iron, scattered herbs, crumbs — and a faint wisp of steam carrying the [SEASON/MOOD]; let highlights glisten on the food while the surroundings fall into deep, controlled shadow with warm low-key tones; keep the hero food sharp, richly textured and appetising, with intentional negative space and a painterly, Old-Master feel. CONSTRAINTS: keep food realistic and edible-looking; rich blacks but the dish never lost to darkness; believable single-source lighting (no flat AI glow); natural colours warmed by the light, not artificially saturated; no added text or logos; tasteful, uncluttered styling. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished moody food image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to lift the shadows, change the props or shift the season.

Title: Bright & Fresh Clean-Eating Lifestyle Shot
Teaser: The opposite of moody — airy, sun-washed and wholesome, with white surfaces, fresh produce and soft daylight. The healthy, breezy look for salads, smoothies, breakfast bowls and clean-label brands.
PromptHint: Name the [DISH], optionally set the [SETTING] (e.g. sunlit kitchen, bright cafe table) and an [ACCENT COLOR] from the ingredients.
Prompt: ROLE: You are a lifestyle food photographer shooting a bright, fresh, health-forward image. TASK: Render the [DISH] in a clean, airy, sunlit scene that feels wholesome and energising. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the time of day for the cleanest daylight, which fresh ingredients add a pop of [ACCENT COLOR], and how to keep the frame light and uncluttered. THEN GENERATE: place the [DISH] on a light surface within [SETTING], bathed in soft, bright natural daylight with gentle shadows and an airy high-key feel; scatter a few fresh whole ingredients — leaves, fruit, seeds — for colour and authenticity; keep whites clean, greens vivid and the food crisp, dewy and just-prepared; use a shallow depth of field so the dish stays sharp while the bright background softens, leaving calm negative space. CONSTRAINTS: keep food fresh, realistic and appetising — no wilting, no plastic gloss; clean true-to-life colours (bright but not blown out or neon); believable soft daylight; no added text or logos; uncluttered, breezy styling. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished fresh lifestyle image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting, lighten the mood further, or swap an ingredient.

Title: Hands-In-Action Cooking & Plating Moment
Teaser: Captures the human moment — hands kneading dough, drizzling sauce, sprinkling flaky salt from above, or plating the final garnish. The 'made with care' story shot that gives food warmth and authenticity.
PromptHint: Name the [DISH OR FOOD], describe the [ACTION] the hands are doing, and optionally the [SETTING].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an editorial food photographer capturing the human craft behind a dish. TASK: Show a pair of hands performing the [ACTION] with the [DISH OR FOOD], caught mid-moment as if for a story spread. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the one gesture that best shows care and craft, the angle that keeps both the hands and the food readable, and the light that makes the food the focal point. THEN GENERATE: frame the [DISH OR FOOD] as the clear hero with realistic hands mid-[ACTION] — drizzling, sprinkling, garnishing, kneading or pouring — within a softly blurred [SETTING] of a real kitchen or counter; light it with warm directional window light, gentle shadows and a candid, unstaged feel; keep the food and the active gesture sharp and appetising while the background falls into a creamy shallow-depth blur; capture a falling sprinkle, drip or wisp of steam to add life and motion. CONSTRAINTS: show natural five-finger hands with believable skin and no extra fingers; keep food realistic and appetising; authentic candid lighting (no stiff stock-photo posing); no added text or logos; one clear action. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished hands-in-action image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the action, setting or time of day.

Title: Premium Food Packaging Mockup On A Styled Set
Teaser: Drops your packaged product into a believable, appetising scene — the box, jar, bag or bottle hero-lit beside its actual ingredients on a styled surface. The packshot that makes a shelf product look craveable and premium.
PromptHint: Name the [PACKAGED PRODUCT] (e.g. coffee bag, jam jar, chocolate bar), list the [INGREDIENTS] to style around it, and optionally a [SURFACE/PALETTE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a commercial packaging photographer shooting a premium food product for retail and ads. TASK: Render the [PACKAGED PRODUCT] as a hero packshot styled on a believable set with its real ingredients. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the angle that flatters the package shape and shows the front label clearly, where to place the supporting ingredients, and how to keep the package the obvious hero. THEN GENERATE: place the [PACKAGED PRODUCT] front-and-centre with the label crisp and legible, styled on a [SURFACE/PALETTE] with the [INGREDIENTS] artfully arranged nearby — beans, fruit, grain, spilled product — to signal flavour and quality; light it with a soft key plus a clean edge highlight to separate it from the background, add a natural contact shadow and a subtle surface reflection; keep the packaging materials photoreal (matte, gloss, foil, glass, kraft paper) and tack-sharp with a shallow depth of field, leaving calm negative space for a headline. CONSTRAINTS: keep the package's real shape, colours and any visible branding accurate — do not invent a different product; keep ingredients realistic; no warped or melted labels; no added text or logos beyond what's on the package; one product as the hero; believable studio lighting. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished packaging image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface, swap an ingredient or adjust the angle.

Title: Step-By-Step Recipe Card Grid
Teaser: Turns a recipe into a clean, consistent visual sequence — three to six matched panels showing each key stage in the same light and framing. The shop-the-steps look for blogs, cookbooks and how-to reels.
PromptHint: Name the [RECIPE], list the [KEY STEPS] in order (3-6 of them), and optionally a [SURFACE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a food photographer producing a clean, consistent step-by-step recipe sequence. TASK: Create one image laid out as a tidy grid of matched panels showing the [KEY STEPS] of the [RECIPE] in order. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how many panels the steps need, keep camera angle, distance, surface and lighting identical across every panel so the sequence reads as one set, and decide which gesture best shows each step. THEN GENERATE: render the steps as equal-sized panels in reading order, each shot from the same overhead or three-quarter angle on the same [SURFACE] with identical soft, even lighting; show the food progressing realistically from raw to finished — mixing, chopping, layering, cooking, plating — with hands acting where it helps; keep each panel crisp, appetising and colour-consistent, separated by thin clean dividers, with small legible step numbers in a clean sans-serif corner badge. CONSTRAINTS: keep food and progression realistic and continuous (the dish must look like the same recipe throughout); identical framing and light across panels; correctly rendered numbers only, no other text; natural five-finger hands if shown; no logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished recipe-grid image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the number of steps, the surface or the camera angle.

Title: Cinematic Slow-Motion Food Reel Frame
Teaser: A single frame that looks pulled from a high-end food film — shallow depth, a cheese pull or honey drizzle caught mid-stretch, warm bokeh and that buttery commercial-grade light. The look that makes a clip feel premium before a word is spoken.
PromptHint: Name the [DISH], describe the [HERO MOMENT] (e.g. cheese pull, sauce drizzle, steam rising), and optionally a [COLOR GRADE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a food commercial cinematographer framing a single still from a premium food film. TASK: Render one cinematic frame of the [DISH] caught at its [HERO MOMENT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the moment of peak appetite appeal, the lens feel (shallow depth, gentle compression) and the colour grade that gives it a polished commercial mood. THEN GENERATE: frame the [DISH] with a shallow cinematic depth of field and soft, buttery key light, capturing the [HERO MOMENT] mid-action — a slow cheese pull, a glossy drizzle in motion, rising steam or a pour caught mid-stream; render warm bokeh in the background, gentle film grain and a [COLOR GRADE] that feels rich and intentional; keep the food tack-sharp at the focal plane, appetising and glistening, with a widescreen, story-driven composition. CONSTRAINTS: keep food realistic and appetising; physically believable motion (pulls, drips and steam that obey gravity); cinematic but natural colour (no over-graded neon); no added text or logos; one clear hero moment. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished cinematic food frame only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 16:9 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the hero moment or the colour grade.

Title: Dessert & Pastry Beauty Shot With Drips And Dust
Teaser: The indulgent close-up that makes a sweet tooth ache — molten chocolate running down a slice, a dusting of sugar caught in the light, a glossy glaze catching a highlight. The decadent hero shot for bakeries and dessert menus.
PromptHint: Name the [DESSERT], describe the [INDULGENT DETAIL] (e.g. chocolate drip, powdered-sugar dust, fruit coulis), and optionally a [BACKDROP].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a pastry and dessert photographer shooting an indulgent beauty shot for a bakery. TASK: Render the [DESSERT] as a decadent hero close-up that maximises temptation. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the angle that shows layers, height and gloss, where the [INDULGENT DETAIL] should catch the light, and how to make it look fresh and just-finished. THEN GENERATE: frame the [DESSERT] close and slightly above the surface against a soft [BACKDROP], showing the [INDULGENT DETAIL] — a slow molten drip, glossy glaze, a falling dust of powdered sugar or cocoa, a spoonful breaking into a soft centre — caught at its most tempting; light it with a soft directional key for glossy highlights and gentle shadow, plus a touch of backlight to make sugar dust and glaze sparkle; keep textures rich and tactile (crumb, cream, ganache, flake) and tack-sharp with a luscious shallow depth of field. CONSTRAINTS: keep the dessert realistic and edible-looking — believable drips, dust and glaze that obey gravity, no plastic gloss; natural rich colours (not artificially saturated); no added text or logos; one indulgent hero. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished dessert image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the indulgent detail, backdrop or angle.

Title: Branded Social Ad Layout With Headline & Offer Space
Teaser: A complete, ready-to-run food ad frame — appetising dish on one side, a designated headline zone and offer-badge spot on the other, balanced and on-brand for a single static creative you can drop straight into a campaign.
PromptHint: Name the [DISH OR PRODUCT], set a [BRAND COLOR], optionally note the [OFFER] the badge should leave room for, and the [ASPECT RATIO].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an art director designing a ready-to-run static food ad creative. TASK: Build one balanced ad layout that pairs an appetising shot of the [DISH OR PRODUCT] with clean space reserved for a headline and an offer badge. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide which side holds the food and which holds the copy, how the [BRAND COLOR] should anchor the design, and where an [OFFER] badge would sit without crowding the food. THEN GENERATE: place a mouth-watering, well-lit shot of the [DISH OR PRODUCT] on one half of the frame against a clean background tinted toward [BRAND COLOR]; reserve a calm, uncluttered zone on the other half for a headline, plus a clear circular or pill-shaped area where an offer badge will go; light the food with a soft key and appetising highlights, add a natural contact shadow, and keep the overall composition balanced, on-brand and campaign-ready with deliberate negative space. CONSTRAINTS: keep the food realistic and appetising; leave the headline and badge zones genuinely empty (no placeholder lorem text, no invented logos or fake percentages); brand-colour background must not bleed onto the food; one hero dish; believable lighting. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished ad-layout image only in [ASPECT RATIO], then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to flip the layout, change the brand colour or resize the offer-badge zone.
